name: Ummey Hani Barsha
title: Ph.D. Candidate, Consumer and Design Sciences · Auburn University
bio: My professional goal is to become an academic scholar specializing in sustainability issues within the textile and fashion industry. I aim to contribute to fostering sustainability in the textile and fashion industry by focusing on three interconnected areas: (1) promoting sustainable materials and manufacturing processes, (2) understanding consumption patterns and addressing the wearer's experience, and (3) developing sustainable apparel design techniques through research and teaching. This pursuit not only fulfills my personal ambition but also reflects my moral commitment to advancing a field with profound global consequences.

professional_affiliations.organization: Institution of Textile Engineers and Technologists (ITET)

service.role: Fashion Event Judge, Behind the Seams

outreach.activity: FYE Mentor, Graduate Student Council First-Year Experience Program
outreach.description: Facilitated conversations with first-year graduate students and guided accommodation, transportation, tutoring resources, and campus involvement opportunities to support a smooth transition to life in Auburn.

awards.name: College of Human Sciences Graduate Research Fellowship
